attack_surface: Agent config persistence (electron-store IPC bridge), context display metrics, settings tab ordering — targeting restart/retry/duplicate-effect/partial-write/stale-state/concurrent-modification paths across main process (ipc.ts), preload bridge (preload/index.ts, preload/types.ts), and renderer components (dialog-manage-agents.tsx, dialog-edit-agent.tsx, session-context-usage.tsx, session-context-metrics.ts)
attacks_attempted: Restart safety: verify electron-store persists desktop-custom-agents across app restarts, check getStore lazy init ordering, verify loaded() guard reloads correctly on dialog remount

Duplicate-effect: test rapid double-click on Create — verify dialog.show() blocks concurrent openings; test rapid double-confirm on Delete — verify signal guard (confirmDelete) prevents no-op second delete; test duplicate Edit saves — verify map-by-id idempotency
Partial write: verify saveCustomAgents awaits IPC result; verify error feedback on IPC failure; check local state rollback on write failure; check electron-store.set() atomicity
Stale state: verify customAgents signal reloads when dialog reopens; check builtinAgents memo reactively tracks sync.data.agent changes; check context metrics re-evaluate on message/provider changes
Concurrent modification: verify dialog stacking behavior (dispose parent on child open); test IPC response ordering for two rapid saves; check for lost updates when IPC responses arrive out of order

Corrupt data propagation: verify schema validation on get-desktop-custom-agents IPC read; verify schema validation on set-desktop-custom-agents IPC write; check for data versioning/migration mechanism
Unhandled promise rejection: verify .catch() on getCustomAgents IPC call; verify .catch() on setCustomAgents IPC call; verify error toast or fallback on IPC failure
Dialog lifecycle zombie: verify onSave closure survives parent dialog disposal; verify signal reads from disposed owner return correct values; verify IPC write fires from disposed context

findings.description: saveCustomAgents (line 93-96) fires IPC setCustomAgents without await/then/catch. On IPC failure (disk full, permissions, serialization): (1) local state is already updated, (2) success toast fires immediately (line 104/118/135), (3) persistence fails silently. User sees 'saved' but data is gone on restart. The ipcMain.handle at ipc.ts line 224-226 also lacks try/catch — any store.set() exception propagates as an unhandled promise rejection to the renderer.
findings.repair_instruction: Make saveCustomAgents async: await the IPC call. Wrap in try/catch. On catch: revert local state via setCustomAgents to previous value (capture before update), show error toast instead of success toast. Add try/catch in ipc.ts set-desktop-custom-agents handler to log and rethrow.

findings.description: Both get-desktop-custom-agents and set-desktop-custom-agents IPC handlers (ipc.ts lines 220-227) work with unknown[] without schema validation. Store.get('agents') returns raw JSON; store.set('agents', agents) accepts any unknown[]. If the store file is corrupted, manually edited, or a future version changes AgentDef shape, corrupted/version-mismatched data silently produces undefined fields or garbage AgentDef objects in the renderer. The (agents ?? []) as AgentDef[] cast at dialog-manage-agents.tsx line 59 is an unsound type assertion.
findings.repair_instruction: Add Zod schema or branded type for AgentDef on both read and write IPC handlers. On read: validate each entry, drop/fix corrupted entries, log warning. On write: validate before persisting, reject with descriptive error. Add a store version key ('_version') to enable future migrations.

findings.description: getCustomAgents promise (line 58) has no .catch() handler. On IPC failure: loaded() was already set to true (line 57), so the load guard is skipped for the component lifetime. customAgents stays at [], user sees no custom agents with no error feedback. Natural recovery exists (reopen dialog re-mounts the component), but transient IPC failures are silent.
findings.repair_instruction: Add .catch() handler: show error toast, set loaded(false) to allow retry, or set a fallback empty array explicitly.

findings.description: saveCustomAgents sends the full state array with each IPC call. If two saves occur in rapid succession (e.g., user edits agent A, saves, then immediately edits agent B and saves), IPC responses can arrive out of order. The earlier save's IPC response could overwrite the later save's data in the store. Low probability in single-window desktop app, but no ordering guard exists.
findings.repair_instruction: Add a monotonic sequence counter or timestamp to each saveCustomAgents call. In the ipc.ts handler, only apply the write if the counter/timestamp is newer than the last applied write. Alternatively, use a debounce pattern: batch rapid saves into a single write.

findings.description: Type mismatch between ElectronAPI (preload/types.ts line 109-110: Promise<AgentDef[]>) and the ambient Window.api declaration (app.tsx lines 79-80: Promise<unknown[]>) and dialog-manage-agents.tsx local api() type (lines 14-19: Promise<unknown[]>). The (agents ?? []) as AgentDef[] cast at line 59 masks runtime type errors. If a future preload version returns a different shape, the cast will silently produce garbage AgentDef objects instead of a type error.
findings.repair_instruction: Align types: declare getCustomAgents/setCustomAgents with AgentDef[] consistently across app.tsx, preload/types.ts, and dialog-manage-agents.tsx. Remove the unsound as AgentDef[] cast. Import AgentDef from a shared source rather than re-declaring it in dialog-manage-agents.tsx.
